notes: Split into HP Inc. and HPE in 2015; HP-UX reached end of support Dec 2025
RS/6000 line rebranded as IBM eServer pSeries, then IBM Power Systems; AIX still active
Acquired by Oracle for $7.4B in January 2010; Solaris/SPARC continued under Oracle
Acquired by Compaq for $9.6B in June 1998; Compaq later acquired by HP in 2002
Siemens and Fujitsu merged computer operations to form Fujitsu Siemens Computers in 1999
French IT company; RISC/Unix line discontinued; merged enterprise services into Atos
Acquired by EMC in 1999 for $1.1B; DG/UX Unix OS discontinued
Acquired by Compaq in 1997; NonStop systems eventually moved to HP after HP-Compaq merger
Became Fujitsu ICL then Fujitsu Services; brand discontinued
Filed Chapter 11 in 2009; assets acquired by Rackable Systems which renamed itself SGI
Hitachi RS/6000-compatible systems were a niche; Hitachi still active as diversified conglomerate
Acquired by Siemens AG in March 1995 for ~$207M; merged into Siemens Computer Systems
NCR Teradata data warehousing business spun off as Teradata 2007; NCR itself still active
Acquired by IBM in 1999; NUMA technology incorporated into IBM xSeries servers
Aberdeen Group acquired by Harte-Hanks in 2001; brand continued as Aberdeen Group through 2010s
Windows NT Server line evolved into Windows 2000 Server, Windows Server 2003 and beyond
IA-64 (Itanium) released in 2001 but never achieved projected dominance; Intel x86-64 prevailed
